Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a normative process description and an event log with 5 cases We need to identify which cases deviate from the intended process flow and explain the violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Main points Process always begins with Receive order After receiving next step is Check inventory must occur before any decisions about payment authorization or fulfillment If inventory sufficient sequence Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order If inventory insufficient after Check inventory branch to Cancel order then Close order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key constraints Authorize payment should only occur after Check inventory and before packaging Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Ship order only after packaging and never after cancellation Close order final

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order This follows the sufficient inventory path exactly No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Here after Check inventory they did Package items BEFORE Authorize payment The normative says Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping So case 2 violates the rule that packaging must be after payment authorization

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order This is odd The normative mentions that if inventory insufficient it should go to Cancel order directly after Check without Authorize payment So Authorize payment should not occur in the insufficient inventory case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: After Cancel they Ship order which is explicitly prohibited Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation So Case 3 violates authorization before a cancellation path and shipping after cancellation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order This follows the insufficient inventory path perfectly No violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Missing Package items The sequence jumps from Authorize payment directly to Ship order The normative says Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Thus Case 5 violates that requirement
